name: Google Cloud Rapid Agent Hackathon
amount: Up to $5,000 per track (1st place); $60,000 total prize pool across 6 partner tracks
eligibility: Individuals and teams above legal age of majority; globally open with some country exclusions; must build using Google Cloud Agent Builder with Gemini
description: Build AI agents using Google Cloud Agent Builder + Gemini and compete for a share of $60K in cash across six partner-technology tracks (Arize, Elastic, Fivetran, GitLab, MongoDB, Dynatrace). Each track pays $5K/$3K/$2K for 1st/2nd/3rd. Judged on technical implementation (30%), business case (30%), innovation (20%), and demo (20%). Agents must tackle a real-world challenge via tool integration. 12,000+ participants already registered.
viabilityNote: Free entry, real cash prizes, globally open. June 11, 2026 deadline — submit now.

name: ACL Caregiver AI Prize Challenge
amount: Up to $100,000 per winner in Phase 1; up to $2.5 million total prize funding across all phases
eligibility: Any team or organization globally may participate; prize awards limited to U.S. citizens and permanent residents; must build AI tools for family caregiver support
description: Federal prize competition to develop AI tools that ease the burden on family caregivers. Three-phase competition: Phase 1 (due July 31, 2026) awards up to 10 prizes of $100K each per track, plus meritorious prizes of $50K. Phases 2 and 3 dates to be announced. Focus areas: care navigation, coordination, emotional support, and crisis triage for caregivers of older adults and persons with disabilities.
viabilityNote: Prize awards limited to US citizens/residents, but global teams can participate. Phase 1 requires a design/feasibility submission by July 31, 2026. Apply early.
relevant2026Note: Phase 1 applications due July 31, 2026. Actively open now.

name: OpenAI Safety Fellowship
deadline: Applications for Sep 2026 cohort closed May 3, 2026; watch for next cycle
eligibility: External researchers, engineers, and practitioners in CS, social science, cybersecurity, HCI, or related fields; global applicants welcome; no OpenAI affiliation required; research ability prioritized over credentials
description: A 5-month external research fellowship run by OpenAI for independent AI safety researchers. Fellows receive a weekly cash stipend, ~$15K/month compute, and mentorship from OpenAI researchers. Work is in-person in Berkeley (Constellation) or remote. Priority areas: safety evaluation, ethics, robustness, agentic oversight, and high-severity misuse domains. 80%+ of fellows produce a paper, benchmark, or dataset by end of the program.
viabilityNote: Substantial weekly cash stipend for independent AI safety researchers. Sep 2026 cohort apps closed May 3 — watch openai.com for the next annual cycle.
relevant2026Note: Current cohort runs Sep 14, 2026 – Feb 5, 2027. Applications for this cohort closed May 3, 2026; next cycle expected to open in late 2026 or early 2027.

name: Pivotal Research Fellowship
organization: Pivotal Research (London Initiative for Safe AI)
amount: £6,000–£8,000 stipend + £2,000 housing support + weekday meals + compute; 9-week program
deadline: Q3 2026 closed May 3, 2026; Q4 2026 applications expected September 2026
eligibility: Anyone 18+ serious about AI safety research — undergraduates through PhDs to experienced professionals; no prior AI safety background required; in-person in London required; global applicants welcome
description: A quarterly 9-week AI safety research fellowship hosted at the London Initiative for Safe AI (LISA). Fellows work with senior mentors on alignment, interpretability, security, and governance. Full financial support: cash stipend, housing, weekday meals, and compute. 70–90% of fellows receive optional 6-month extensions. Cohorts of ~25 peers per quarter.
viabilityNote: Q3 2026 apps closed May 3. Express interest for Q4 at pivotal-research.org. Fully funded, real cash stipend, strong fit for individuals pursuing AI safety research.
relevant2026Note: Q3 cohort (June 29–Aug 28, 2026) apps closed May 3. Q4 2026 cohort expected to open applications in September 2026.

name: Zendesk Tech for Good Impact Awards
amount: $5,000–$50,000 cash grant + free Zendesk AI-powered platform access
eligibility: Registered nonprofits globally (geographic exclusions apply: Belarus, Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Russia, Syria, Ukraine, and others); must not be current Zendesk customers or prior Zendesk grantees; focus on community resilience, crisis support, or career pathways into tech
description: Annual cash grants plus free access to Zendesk's AI-powered customer service and Resolution Platform for nonprofits doing high-impact work. Three focus areas: foster community connections, promote resilience in crisis, and create tech career pathways. Cash awards of $5K–$50K, with recipients also receiving Zendesk platform software. Global eligibility with some country exclusions. Application via online form.
viabilityNote: Open to nonprofits globally. Cash plus AI platform access. Not for current Zendesk customers. Submit the online form before July 15, 2026.

name: a2 Pilot Awards — AI and Aging
organization: a2 Collective (National Institute on Aging, NIH)
amount: Up to $200,000 non-dilutive grant per project (most projects $50K–$150K); 12-month performance period
deadline: Next competition expected mid-to-late 2026, pending NIA renewal
eligibility: Any U.S. organization with a Unique Entity Identifier (for-profit, nonprofit, university, or government entity); PI does not require PhD or MD; project must apply AI or advanced technology to improve health outcomes for older Americans or their caregivers
description: Annual non-dilutive grant competition funding AI and technology demonstration pilots for older adults and family caregivers, part of NIA's $40M AITC program. Five cohorts have been funded (20–30 awards per year). Current 5-year cycle concluded; new competition expected mid-to-late 2026 contingent on NIA program renewal. Historically funds LLM-based care navigation, AI diagnostic tools, remote monitoring, and caregiver support platforms.
viabilityNote: US-only; must focus on AI for aging or caregiving. Current 5th cycle closed. Sign up at a2collective.ai for notification when the next cycle opens mid-to-late 2026.
relevant2026Note: 5th annual competition closed; next cycle expected mid-to-late 2026 pending NIA renewal. High historical conversion: 20–30 awards per year.
